summarization.a: The chart shows the energy change (ΔE) in kcal/mol for the reaction of ZrCl4 with different substrate complexes labeled A, B, and C.

data_extraction.a:
| Reaction Step | ΔE (kcal/mol) A |ΔE (kcal/mol) B |ΔE (kcal/mol) C |
|---|---|---|---|
| Start | 0 |0 |0 |
| Complex | -17|-21|-28|
| TS | 0| -2|-8|
| HCl (g)| -5| -7|-12|

a.question: Is there faster saturation in the initial cycles of this process?
a.answer: a	Yes there is faster saturation as there is less energy required for adsorption on SiO2 than on ZrO2.

a.question: What substrate should be used to have initial nucleation at lower temperatures?
a.answer: a	SiO2 instead of Si or ZrO2 as this material shows the lowest activation energy.
